therapeutic_communication.description: Attend fully; use open posture and verbal acknowledgments.
therapeutic_communication.example: I hear that you're feeling overwhelmed.
Tell me more about what happened before you felt anxious.

therapeutic_communication.description: Mirror feelings or content to validate.
therapeutic_communication.example: You sound frustrated about the wait.
When you say 'voices,' what are they saying?
Nurse remains present without filling pause after loss disclosure.

therapeutic_communication.description: Review key points to confirm understanding.
therapeutic_communication.example: So tonight you felt hopeless after argument, no plan to act.

therapeutic_communication.description: Expand from narrow complaint to context.
therapeutic_communication.example: Besides sleep, how has mood been this week?
I'll be here with you during the procedure.

communication_barriers.description: Telling patient what to do instead of exploring.
communication_barriers.example: You should just leave that relationship.

communication_barriers.therapeutic_alternative: This sounds really painful for you.
We'll work through this together step by step.

communication_barriers.example: Patient mentions SI — nurse asks about lunch.
communication_barriers.therapeutic_alternative: Direct suicide assessment with caring tone.

communication_scenarios.title: Patient states 'Nobody cares if I live or die'
communication_scenarios.patient_statement: Nobody cares if I live or die.
options: Don't talk like that — you'll be fine
It sounds like you're feeling hopeless. Are you thinking of harming yourself?
I'll come back when you're calmer
communication_scenarios.explanation: Direct, caring suicide inquiry is therapeutic and required.
communication_scenarios.clinical_why: Asking about suicide does not increase risk.

communication_scenarios.title: Angry about wait time
communication_scenarios.patient_statement: This place is a joke! I've been waiting hours!
options: I can see you're frustrated. I'm here to help — tell me what's most urgent right now.
Leave if you don't like it
communication_scenarios.explanation: Acknowledge emotion and redirect to problem-solving.

communication_scenarios.title: Command hallucinations
communication_scenarios.patient_statement: The voices tell me to hurt the nurse.
options: Those voices aren't real — ignore them
You're safe here. Do you feel able to resist those commands right now?
Share this only if asked later
communication_scenarios.explanation: Assess command hallucinations and safety; notify provider.

communication_scenarios.title: Grief disclosure
communication_scenarios.patient_statement: I can't stop crying since my wife died.
options: Therapeutic silence and presence, then reflect feelings
communication_scenarios.explanation: Presence and reflection support grieving process.

communication_scenarios.title: Refusing medication
communication_scenarios.patient_statement: I'm not taking those pills — they're poison.
options: Help me understand your concerns about the medication.
communication_scenarios.explanation: Explore beliefs and collaborate — may need capacity evaluation.
communication_scenarios.clinical_why: Rights and safety balance requires assessment.

safety_risk_flags.finding: Active suicidal ideation with plan and access to means
safety_risk_flags.action: 1:1 observation; remove means; notify provider; document direct quotes

safety_risk_flags.finding: Homicidal ideation with identified victim and plan
safety_risk_flags.action: Notify provider and security; 1:1; duty to warn per state law

safety_risk_flags.finding: Voluntary hold patient attempting to leave unit
safety_risk_flags.action: Follow elopement protocol; notify charge nurse; do not physically restrain without training/order

safety_risk_flags.finding: Escalating aggression with weapons or thrown objects
safety_risk_flags.action: Call team; clear area; de-escalation; seclusion/restraint only as last resort per policy

safety_risk_flags.finding: CIWA-Ar score ≥20 or autonomic hyperactivity with delirium
safety_risk_flags.action: Notify provider; benzodiazepine protocol; continuous monitoring

safety_risk_flags.finding: Agitation, hyperreflexia, clonus, fever after serotonergic drugs
safety_risk_flags.action: Stop serotonergic agents; notify provider; monitor vitals — serotonin syndrome

safety_risk_flags.finding: Neuroleptic malignant syndrome: rigidity, fever, altered mental status on antipsychotic
safety_risk_flags.action: Stop antipsychotic; notify provider; ICU-level care

safety_risk_flags.finding: Fresh cutting or ligature marks with intent to die
safety_risk_flags.action: Safety search; 1:1; wound care; psychiatric evaluation

screening_tools.name: PHQ-9
screening_tools.scoring: 0–27; ≥10 suggests moderate depression; item 9 screens suicide
screening_tools.nursing_action: Escalate positive item 9 immediately; notify provider for high scores

screening_tools.name: GAD-7
screening_tools.nursing_action: Support coping strategies; notify provider for severe scores

screening_tools.name: CIWA-Ar
screening_tools.nursing_action: Benzodiazepine per protocol for elevated scores; q1-2h monitoring if severe

screening_tools.name: COWS
screening_tools.scoring: Aggregated signs — higher = more severe withdrawal
screening_tools.nursing_action: Medication-assisted treatment per provider; comfort measures

disorders.name: Major Depressive Disorder
disorders.summary: Persistent depressed mood or anhedonia ≥2 weeks with functional impairment
disorders.pathophysiology: Monoamine dysregulation; HPA axis changes; neuroplasticity reduction.

disorders.name: Bipolar Disorder
disorders.summary: Episodes of mania/hypomania alternating with depression
disorders.pathophysiology: Dysregulated mood circuits; strong genetic component.

disorders.name: Schizophrenia
disorders.summary: Positive symptoms (hallucinations, delusions) and negative symptoms (flat affect, avolition)

disorders.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder
disorders.summary: Excessive worry ≥6 months with physical symptoms

disorders.name: PTSD
disorders.summary: Trauma exposure with intrusion, avoidance, hyperarousal, negative cognition
disorders.pathophysiology: Hyperactive fear response; altered memory consolidation.

disorders.name: Substance Use Disorder
disorders.summary: Maladaptive use with impairment and craving
disorders.pathophysiology: Reward pathway hijacking; tolerance and withdrawal.

de_escalation.title: Calm Voice and Open Posture
de_escalation.description: Lower voice volume; hands visible; maintain safe distance.

de_escalation.title: Set Clear Limits
de_escalation.description: State expectations calmly: 'I need you to step back so we can talk safely.'

de_escalation.title: Offer Choices
de_escalation.description: Two acceptable options to restore sense of control.

de_escalation.title: Pacing and shouting in hallway
options: Approach with team, offer quiet space and PRN per order
de_escalation.explanation: Least restrictive intervention first with team support.

safety_drill.finding: Patient handed nurse a suicide note and pills
options: Place note in chart and continue rounds
safety_drill.explanation: Imminent risk — continuous observation and means removal.
safety_drill.clinical_why: Written plan with means is highest acute risk.

safety_drill.finding: Patient with history of violence picks up chair
options: Clear others, call team, verbal de-escalation
safety_drill.explanation: Team response and safety of others priority.

options: Notify provider and initiate benzodiazepine protocol

safety_drill.finding: Patient reports command to stab roommate
options: Assess intent/capacity to resist; 1:1; notify provider; separate roommate
safety_drill.explanation: Command hallucinations with identified target = immediate safety.
safety_drill.clinical_why: Duty to protect patient and others.

safety_drill.finding: Lithium level 2.2 mEq/L with coarse tremor and confusion
options: Hold lithium and notify provider immediately

practice_questions.question: Therapeutic response to 'I want to die':
options: Are you having thoughts of killing yourself right now?
practice_questions.explanation: Direct, nonjudgmental suicide inquiry ('Are you thinking of killing yourself right now?') is evidence-based and does not plant the idea.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale and Joint Commission require direct assessment — ambiguous responses miss imminent risk.

practice_questions.question: Non-therapeutic communication is:
practice_questions.explanation: False reassurance ('Everything will be fine') dismisses emotion and shuts down communication — non-therapeutic per Peplau.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Patients stop sharing suicidal thoughts when minimized — increases completed suicide risk.

practice_questions.question: CIWA-Ar score 22 indicates:
practice_questions.explanation: CIWA-Ar ≥20 indicates severe alcohol withdrawal — initiate benzodiazepine protocol (lorazepam/diazepam), thiamine, monitor for seizures and DTs.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Delirium tremens mortality reaches 15% without treatment — CIWA guides symptom-triggered dosing.

practice_questions.question: Priority for command hallucinations to harm others:
practice_questions.explanation: Command hallucinations directing harm require immediate safety assessment: intent, ability to resist, access to weapons, 1:1 observation, notify provider.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Command hallucinations to harm others carry higher violence completion rate than passive ideation.

practice_questions.question: PHQ-9 item 9 positive requires:
practice_questions.explanation: PHQ-9 item 9 asks about suicidal thoughts — any positive score requires immediate risk assessment, safety plan, and possible 1:1.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Screening tools are not substitutes for clinical interview but trigger mandatory follow-up.

practice_questions.question: Serotonin syndrome includes:
practice_questions.explanation: Serotonin syndrome: agitation, hyperreflexia, clonus, hyperthermia, autonomic instability — often from SSRI + MAOI or linezolid. Stop serotonergic drugs, supportive care, cyproheptadine.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Can progress to rhabdomyolysis and death within hours — often mistaken for infection.

practice_questions.question: Least restrictive intervention means:
options: Start with verbal de-escalation and milieu management
practice_questions.explanation: Least restrictive environment: verbal de-escalation, milieu changes, PRN medications before restraint/seclusion — CMS Conditions of Participation.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Restraint causes trauma, positional asphyxia, and death — document alternatives attempted.

practice_questions.question: Lithium teaching includes:
options: Maintain consistent sodium and fluid intake
practice_questions.explanation: Lithium is renally cleared — maintain consistent sodium and fluid intake; dehydration, NSAIDs, and ACE inhibitors raise levels.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Therapeutic index narrow (0.6–1.2 mEq/L) — toxicity causes tremor, confusion, seizures.

practice_questions.question: Trauma-informed care emphasizes:
practice_questions.explanation: Trauma-informed care: safety, trustworthiness, choice, collaboration, empowerment — avoid retraumatization from restraint or forced detail of trauma.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Retraumatization worsens PTSD symptoms and treatment engagement.

practice_questions.question: Antipsychotic monitoring includes:
options: Weight, glucose, lipids for metabolic syndrome
practice_questions.explanation: Second-generation antipsychotics cause metabolic syndrome — monitor weight, waist circumference, fasting glucose, lipids at baseline and ongoing.
practice_questions.clinical_why: Patients with SMI die 25 years earlier — cardiovascular disease from untreated metabolic effects.

practice_questions.question: Restraint/seclusion requires:
options: Provider order, least restrictive alternatives attempted, continuous monitoring
practice_questions.explanation: Restraint/seclusion: physician/provider order within 1 hour, face-to-face evaluation, continuous monitoring, release at earliest safe moment, debrief with patient.
practice_questions.clinical_why: CMS and state regulations limit duration — failure to comply is reportable violation.
